expectations.text: Every function in text_utils.py has a corresponding .py file (9 functions: strip_images, strip_urls, strip_references, strip_acknowledgments, strip_extra_blanks, _split_sections, _classify_headings_with_llm, extract_relevant_sections, preprocess_md)
expectations.evidence: All 9 files present: example_strip_images.py, example_strip_urls.py, example_strip_references.py, example_strip_acknowledgments.py, example_strip_extra_blanks.py, example_split_sections.py, example_classify_headings_with_llm.py, example_extract_relevant_sections.py, example_preprocess_md.py

expectations.text: Every generated .py file runs successfully with `python <file>` (exit code 0)
expectations.evidence: All 9 files executed with exit code 0. Each produced correct output with PASS assertions succeeding.

expectations.text: _classify_headings_with_llm.py uses a mock instead of making real API calls, yet still demonstrates the function's behavior
expectations.evidence: example_classify_headings_with_llm.py uses unittest.mock (patch, MagicMock) to mock openai.OpenAI and dotenv.load_dotenv. A make_mock_client() helper creates mock clients with configurable response content or side_effect. Four examples are demonstrated: typical headings, empty removal, unparseable response, and API failure. No real API calls are made.

expectations.text: Sample inputs use realistic scientific paper markdown content with real-looking headings, image tags, URLs, etc.
expectations.evidence: Files use realistic content: example_strip_images.py has CDN and local image tags with gene text; example_strip_urls.py has NCBI GEO, DOI, and BLAST URLs; example_strip_references.py has realistic citation entries; example_split_sections.py has a paper with Introduction/Results/Methods/References; example_preprocess_md.py demonstrates the full pipeline on a paper with images, URLs, and multiple sections about OsCHI and flavonoid metabolism.

expectations.text: Each file has a docstring explaining the function's purpose
expectations.evidence: Every file has a module-level docstring explaining the function name, what it does, its signature, and usage notes (e.g., that it uses mocks for API calls). Examples: 'Example: strip_images() -- Removes Markdown image tags from text', 'Example: _classify_headings_with_llm() -- Calls an LLM API to determine which section headings should be removed'.
